digest.top_line: NRG-GU005: SBRT superiority not met (5yr DFS HR 1.38, p=0.13) but bowel QoL favored SBRT (35% vs 44% MCID, p=0.034).
digest.tldr: Prostate carried the day: all four reads are on fractionation and salvage. NRG-GU005 and HYDRA converge, hypofractionation buys no efficacy edge over standard, so toxicity/QoL decide. SBRT wins bowel QoL, dose-escalated MHFRT adds late GI harm. Meier's 10-yr SBRT durability and the ARS reirradiation consensus round it out.

sites.intro: All four reads are prostate RT: ultrahypofractionation durability and toxicity, moderate-hypofractionation dose selection, and salvage reirradiation for local radiorecurrence.

studies.name: ARS Appropriate Use Criteria: Intraprostatic Recurrence
studies.tldr: PSMA-PET + mpMRI staging, mandatory biopsy, then curative-intent salvage reirradiation (≤6 fractions) preferred over ADT alone for local intraprostatic radiorecurrence.
details: 🔍 ARS Genitourinary Appropriate Use Criteria; modified Delphi consensus across 4 topics: staging, biopsy, salvage selection, reRT technique
🔍 Staging: PSMA-PET + mpMRI to exclude occult metastatic disease and map local extent before salvage
🔍 Biopsy mandatory before local salvage, to avoid treating a radiographic recurrence that is only treatment effect (excess toxicity)
Local salvage preferred over noncurative hormonal manipulation alone; shared decision-making stressed
💊 ADT for radiosensitization only short-course; classic ADT preferred over novel hormonal agents (ARSIs)
📊 Most reRT salvage regimens deliverable in ≤6 fractions; whole-gland salvage toxicity called very tolerable

details.text: 📊 FORECAST (n=155): mpMRI vs MRI-targeted biopsy for radiorecurrence (systematic biopsy reference)
🔗 RTOG 9408 post-hoc: positive post-RT biopsy (n=831) linked to HR 1.7 for BCR, independent of upfront ADT

details: ⚠️ Evidence limited to conventionally fractionated EBRT with pre-PSMA-PET-era workup; no prospective salvage RCT
studies.significance: For the salvage decision after definitive RT: biopsy is mandatory before reirradiation (positive post-RT biopsy carried HR 1.7 for BCR, RTOG 9408), and local salvage is preferred over ADT alone. Technique note: most reRT regimens run in ≤6 fractions, and classic ADT (not ARSIs) is the preferred short-course radiosensitizer.
open_questions: Prospective salvage reirradiation trials to define optimal regimen
Best focal vs whole-gland salvage after PSMA-PET-era staging
Role of ADT duration/type as radiosensitizer in salvage reRT

These patients respond well to re-irradiation using brachytherapy with about 50% of selected patients remaining free of recurrence 5 years after salvage. studies.name: HYDRA (MARCAP Consortium)
studies.tldr: No PFS difference for isodose or dose-escalated MHFRT vs CFRT (HR 0.92, 0.94); only dose-escalated adds late G≥2 GI toxicity (OR 1.48).
details: 🔍 IPD meta-analysis of 7 randomised phase III CFRT vs MHFRT trials (MARCAP Consortium), data published pre-Dec 15 2023
🔍 Isodose comparison: 3 trials, n=3454. Dose-escalated comparison: 4 trials, n=2426
🔍 Median f/u 5.4y (IQR 4.6-7.2) isodose, 7.1y (IQR 5.7-8.4) dose-escalated

details.text: 📊 Isodose vs dose-escalated MHFRT, each pooled against CFRT (efficacy, physician toxicity, QOL)

details: 📊 Both MHFRT approaches non-inferior on PFS and neutral on GU; only dose-escalated crossed significance, and only for the GI/bowel axis
🔗 Authors' bottom line: isodose (e.g. 60Gy/20fx) should be the standard MHFRT regimen for localized prostate
⚠️ Isodose vs dose-escalated is an indirect comparison across separate trial sets (different eras, populations, f/u), not a randomised head-to-head
⚠️ Efficacy endpoint is PFS only; no OS or metastasis-specific readout in source

verdict.rationale: High-level IPD pooling of 7 randomised trials; reinforces the already-emerging isodose moderate-hypofrac standard and cautions against dose escalation, rather than establishing a new practice.

studies.significance: The RT-actionable read is regimen choice, not whether to hypofractionate: dose-escalated MHFRT added late G≥2 GI (OR 1.48) and bowel-QOL (OR 1.68) toxicity over CFRT with no PFS gain (HR 0.94), while isodose stayed null on both. Default to isodose 60Gy/20fx, not a dose-escalated hypofractionated schedule.
open_questions: Isodose vs dose-escalated MHFRT direct head-to-head trial
OS and metastasis-free survival beyond PFS

studies.name: 10-yr SBRT for Prostate Cancer (Meier et al.)
studies.tldr: 10-yr RFS 90% (94% LR, 86% IR); late G3 GI/GU 1.4%/1.5%, no G4-5, after 40Gy/5fx prostate SBRT, no ADT.
details: 📐 40 Gy in 5 fx (8Gy×5), noncoplanar robotic platform + real-time motion mgmt; ~EQD2 100 Gy (α/β=2)
🔍 Phase 2 nonrandomized, N=310 (172 LR, 138 IR), 21 centers, median f/u 9 yr, treated 2008-2010
🔍 Eligibility: LR (T1b-T2a, GS6, PSA≤10) / IR (GS7 & PSA≤10, or GS6 & PSA 10-20); prostate ≤100cc
💊 No androgen suppression allowed, so the cancer-control result is RT-attributable, no hormonal confounding
🔗 Extends PACE-B (SBRT noninferior to conventional EBRT at 5 yr) to 10-yr durability
⚠️ Single-arm, no randomized comparator; physician-reported CTCAE likely underreports late GU vs patient-reported
⚠️ Unfavorable IR is the weak spot: SBRT monotherapy without ADT may undertreat this subgroup

verdict.rationale: Single-arm nonrandomized phase 2, no comparator arm; single-arm design caps the verdict despite mature 10-yr f/u. Aligns with PACE-B 5-yr SBRT noninferiority.
verdict.audience: Localized low- and intermediate-risk prostate, ADT-naive

studies.significance: Unfavorable-IR RFS was 77% vs 92% favorable IR (90% overall), all on SBRT monotherapy without ADT. The 40Gy/5fx robotic regimen holds for LR and favorable IR at 10y; the unfavorable-IR gap is where ADT or intensification still earns its place. Late G2+ GU 14% is the durable cost.
open_questions: ADT benefit for unfavorable intermediate-risk treated with SBRT

studies.name: NRG-GU005
studies.tldr: Co-primary DFS superiority for SBRT not met (HR 1.38, p=0.13); bowel QoL favored SBRT (35% vs 44% MCID, p=0.034).

details.text: 🔍 Phase III international non-blinded RCT, N=698 (353 SBRT / 345 MH-IMRT), localized intermediate-risk prostate, 1:1, powered for SBRT superiority on co-primary DFS + EPIC-26 bowel/urinary HRQOL

details: 🔍 SBRT arm: 36.25 Gy in 5 fx (7.25 Gy/fx), 2-3 fx/wk; PTV margin 5mm radial, 3mm post/ant; fiducials + MRI fusion + daily IGRT mandated
🔍 MH-IMRT arm: 70 Gy/28 fx or 60 Gy/20 fx; PTV margin 8mm except 5mm posteriorly. CTV = prostate ±1cm proximal SV both arms
⚠️ Trial's own goal was SBRT superiority; DFS futility bound crossed at interim and every efficacy endpoint numerically favored IMRT, so no efficacy case for SBRT here

details.text: 📊 EPIC-26 minimal clinically important decline (MCID), SBRT vs MH-IMRT
🔍 Rectal spacer/manipulation used in ~55-56% of both arms (mostly SpaceOAR); associated with further superior EPIC bowel scores (LS mean -2.81, 95% CI -4.49 to -1.13, p=0.0011)

details: 🔗 vs PACE-B: consistent lower urinary-incontinence QoL decline with SBRT vs MH-IMRT despite differing MCID definitions

verdict.rationale: Phase 3 RCT; SBRT already guideline-endorsed. DFS superiority not met (futility) but efficacy similar and bowel QoL co-primary favored SBRT. Consistent with PACE-B.

studies.significance: The additive caution is efficacy: 3-yr biochemical failure doubled with SBRT (8% vs 4%, p=0.037) and 5-yr DFS numerically favored IMRT, sitting under a QoL-favorable headline. SBRT buys a real toxicity/QoL edge (G≥3 GU 0.6% vs 2.5%) but no efficacy gain, so the SBRT-vs-IMRT choice turns on toxicity, not control.
open_questions: Does SBRT's higher 3-yr biochemical failure translate to worse long-term clinical outcomes?
Durability of SBRT QoL benefit and late toxicity beyond 2 years

related_trials.brief_title: Stereotactic Body Radiation Therapy or Intensity-Modulated Radiation Therapy in Treating Patients With Stage IIA-B Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary: This randomized phase III trial studies how well stereotactic body radiation therapy works compared to intensity-modulated radiation therapy in treating patients with stage IIA-B prostate cancer. Radiation therapy uses high energy x-rays to kill tumor cells and shrink tumors. Stereotactic body radiation therapy uses special equipment to position a patient and deliver radiation to tumors with high precision. This method can kill tumor cells with fewer doses over a shorter period and cause less…

related_trials.brief_title: Side Effects of Low Dose Rate Brachytherapy and Ultra-hypofractionated Radiotherapy in Low to Intermediate Risk Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary:
The LDR BURST trial will compare the side effects of two radiotherapy treatments for men with localised prostate cancer. The first treatment is 4D low dose rate brachytherapy where small radioactive seeds are inserted into the prostate gland under ultrasound guidance. A computerised tomography scan is subsequently performed to check seed positioning. Patients usually return home the same day. The seeds emit their radiation over several months before losing their activity.
